description: Legal document from Fustat. Dated: 969 CE. A loan deed describing two compounds owned by Jews ‘located in front of the idolatry (i.e., church) of Bū Serga’ (snippet translated in Yagur, "Living in the City: Jews and Their Residences in Medieval Fusṭāṭ," The Medieval Journal 27 (2024). Each compound is pledged for a debt of 50 dinars. Published in S. Assaf, "Shetarot ʿatiqim," Tarbiz 9 (1937), 196-218. Goitein uses this deed as an example of the confusing scribal "predilection for variety ... especially in names" (Med. Soc. IV p. 279 and VI, 11 n.43).
description_he: מסמך משפטי בעברית מהמאה ה-10. מוחזק בספריית קיימברידג'.
